consistency_model.notes: One in-memory authority; no distributed replication or persistence semantics.

routing_strategy.objective: Establish the latency floor before any distributed indirection.

failure_model.focus: Local process overload and mutex contention.

cost_model.dominant_axis: CPU and memory on one node.
cost_model.notes: No network egress or external service cost yet.

synthesis.scaling_inflection: Shared in-memory state is fast but non-durable and non-scalable.
synthesis.learning_goal: Establish the baseline latency floor and the first comparable workload.

consistency_model.notes: The primary server acknowledges after durable write on one database.

routing_strategy.policy: Direct client-to-single-node routing with synchronous write-through to storage.
routing_strategy.objective: Measure durability cost before introducing horizontal distribution.

failure_model.focus: Write amplification, database stalls, and durable-path errors.

cost_model.notes: Persistence introduces storage and connection overhead.

synthesis.scaling_inflection: Durability improves correctness but adds a measurable synchronous write tax.
synthesis.learning_goal: Make consistency and storage cost explicit before scaling out.

consistency_model.notes: Redis pub/sub favors fast fan-out over durable cross-node ordering guarantees.

routing_strategy.policy: Shared pub/sub bus with node-local websocket fan-out.
routing_strategy.objective: Decouple websocket ingress from cluster-wide fan-out.

failure_model.focus: Broker disconnects, duplicate fan-out, and subscriber lag.

cost_model.dominant_axis: Broker CPU, memory, and cross-node network traffic.
cost_model.notes: Horizontal fan-out shifts cost from one process to the messaging layer.

synthesis.scaling_inflection: Fan-out scales better than a mutex-bound monolith, but consistency becomes eventual.
synthesis.learning_goal: Introduce the first distributed coordination trade-off.

consistency_model.target: Immediate single-node visibility with queued internal processing
consistency_model.notes: Ordering is local to one node, but work now flows through explicit backpressure boundaries.

routing_strategy.policy: Single ingress node with internal worker-pool dispatch.
routing_strategy.objective: Preserve single-node semantics while extending concurrency headroom.

cost_model.dominant_axis: CPU and memory consumed by queue depth and worker concurrency.
cost_model.notes: Cost stays local, but burst handling improves.

synthesis.scaling_inflection: Queueing delays replace lock contention as the dominant bottleneck.
synthesis.learning_goal: Formalize backpressure before moving into cloud-native decomposition.

architecture_summary.scalability: Higher burst tolerance on one node

consistency_model.target: Async durable processing with eventual visibility
consistency_model.notes: Ingest and processing are decoupled; acknowledgement and final visibility may diverge.

routing_strategy.policy: API ingress with queue-based worker dispatch.
routing_strategy.objective: Separate burst absorption from durable processing.

failure_model.focus: Queue buildup, worker slowness, and ingest/worker desynchronization.
mechanisms: Drop accounting and DB error counters

cost_model.dominant_axis: Queue infrastructure, worker CPU, and database writes.
cost_model.notes: Cost shifts toward managed messaging and background processing capacity.

synthesis.scaling_inflection: Burst tolerance improves, but end-to-end semantics are now explicitly asynchronous.
synthesis.learning_goal: Introduce cloud-native decomposition without losing benchmark comparability.

consistency_model.notes: Retries preserve delivery intent, but visibility may be delayed by protection mechanisms.

routing_strategy.policy: API ingress with resilient worker pipeline.
routing_strategy.objective: Trade some latency for higher survival under downstream failure.

failure_model.focus: Circuit breaking, retries, dead-letter routing, and degraded dependencies.

cost_model.dominant_axis: Retry amplification and spare capacity for fault tolerance.
cost_model.notes: Reliability adds operational overhead even when latency stays acceptable.

synthesis.scaling_inflection: Resilience protects throughput during failure, but it must be measured as an explicit trade-off.
synthesis.learning_goal: Make failure handling part of the benchmark contract instead of an afterthought.

consistency_model.target: Mixed model with eventual presence convergence
consistency_model.notes: Presence and typing are intentionally softer-consistency signals than persisted messages.

routing_strategy.policy: Stateful websocket routing with node-local ephemeral coordination.
routing_strategy.objective: Prioritize freshness for high-frequency signals without forcing full durability.

failure_model.focus: Stale presence, fan-out lag, and delivery marker drift.

cost_model.dominant_axis: Memory footprint and websocket session density.
cost_model.notes: Ephemeral real-time state consumes memory before it consumes storage.

synthesis.scaling_inflection: Presence turns state freshness into a first-class scaling problem separate from message durability.
synthesis.learning_goal: Separate soft-state coordination from durable messaging semantics.

architecture_summary.scalability: High websocket density with soft-state routing

consistency_model.notes: Local latency remains fast, while remote visibility is asynchronous and physically bounded.

routing_strategy.policy: Latency-based regional affinity with asynchronous bridge replication.
routing_strategy.objective: Keep local traffic local and push global consistency off the synchronous path.

failure_model.focus: Bridge lag, replication backlog, and regional isolation during partial failure.

cost_model.dominant_axis: Cross-region egress and duplicated regional infrastructure.
cost_model.notes: Geography introduces network and operational cost that scale with replication volume.

synthesis.scaling_inflection: Geography forces a formal consistency trade-off and a formal routing policy.
synthesis.learning_goal: Make regional routing and eventual global convergence explicit.

architecture_summary.scalability: Regional scale with async cross-region replication
architecture_summary.failure_handling: Regional isolation and bridge backlog handling

consistency_model.notes: Security preserves message identity while adding CPU-bound processing before visibility.

routing_strategy.policy: Same regional or local routing as the prior architecture, with crypto validation at ingress.
routing_strategy.objective: Preserve message-path semantics while exposing security cost.

failure_model.focus: Key rotation overhead, replay rejection, and decrypt/signature failures.
mechanisms: Invalid signature and decrypt failure paths

cost_model.dominant_axis: CPU cycles for encryption, decryption, signing, and verification.
cost_model.notes: Security overhead is primarily compute, not transport.

synthesis.scaling_inflection: Confidentiality and integrity change the system budget even when topology stays similar.
synthesis.learning_goal: Make cryptographic cost and failure semantics measurable.

architecture_summary.failure_handling: Replay defense, decrypt failure, key rotation handling

consistency_model.target: Service-coordinated durability with eventual multi-service visibility
consistency_model.notes: The write path is durable, but reads and fan-out now cross service boundaries with explicit trace propagation.

routing_strategy.policy: Gateway-mediated routing with service-specific downstream ownership.
routing_strategy.objective: Make routing decisions explicit as the system decomposes into independently scalable services.

failure_model.focus: Inter-service latency, partial outages, and dependency amplification.

cost_model.dominant_axis: Service-to-service hops, duplicated runtime overhead, and operational complexity.
cost_model.notes: Microservices add coordination and platform cost beyond raw compute.

synthesis.scaling_inflection: The architecture becomes operationally composable, but routing, tracing, and cost must be managed as first-class concerns.
synthesis.learning_goal: End the curriculum with a measurable synthesis of decomposition trade-offs.

architecture_summary.failure_handling: Per-service isolation and degraded dependency handling

consistency_model.notes: The write path is durable and visible through one deployable control plane with metrics and dashboards.

routing_strategy.policy: Gateway-mediated routing with service-specific downstream ownership and standardized control-plane tooling.
routing_strategy.objective: Consolidate the strongest ideas from the previous labs into one deployable local blueprint.

failure_model.focus: Dependency degradation, rate-limit pressure, and service recovery under observability.

cost_model.dominant_axis: Service-to-service hops, database writes, broker overhead, and operational complexity.
cost_model.notes: The blueprint is the most capable stack in the series and intentionally carries the highest platform cost.

synthesis.scaling_inflection: The architecture becomes deployable as a repeatable blueprint, but platform discipline becomes mandatory.
synthesis.learning_goal: End the curriculum with a runnable reference stack that prioritizes clarity, observability, and operational readiness.
